title: Streaming JSON, CSV, Accessible HTML, and Remediation Workflows · Language Lens: Json Stream Contract
summary: Outputs lack version and provenance, CSV formulas stay active, maps randomize order, giant tables fail reflow, and findings have no correction lifecycle. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go json stream contract increment in deterministic accessible report suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Version record envelopes, ordering, missingness, error rows, provenance, encoding, newline, and digest manifests for streaming JSON.
Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Json Stream Contract · Theory Json Stream Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2783 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove streaming JSON, CSV dialect, deterministic order, accessible HTML, and remediation lineage; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
